relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Mercy turns the body into living worship.
Do not let the age shape you; let renewed mind transform perception.
Grace teaches sober self-knowledge according to trust.
One body holds many members and many practices.
In Messiah the many are one body and members of one another.
Grace gives different gifts for faithful expression.
Service and teaching become sacred functions.
Encouragement, generosity, leadership, and mercy each carry their own beauty.
Love must be without mask, clinging to good.
Sibling-love leads in honoring the other.
Let spirit boil with diligent service.
Hope rejoices, pressure endures, prayer perseveres.
Need-sharing and hospitality become embodied mercy.
Bless those pursuing you; refuse the curse.
Let joy and grief be shared.
Humility dissolves self-exalting wisdom.
Do not mirror harm back as harm.
As much as possible, become peaceable with all.
Release vengeance into the Divine field.
Feed and give drink to the enemy, turning fire into conscience.
Do not let harm conquer you; conquer harm with good.

contemplative_note: Romans 12 is the embodied ethic of mercy: transformed mind, gifted body, sincere love, shared grief, hospitality, and harm overcome by good.
